revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hinokuma Shrine]][[ja:日前神宮・國懸神宮]][[simple:Hinokuma Shrine]][[zh:日前宮]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hinokuma Shrine | image = Hinokuma Shrine.JPG | caption = Hinokuma Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.22797|135.20247|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Hinokuma Shrine''' (日前神宮, ''Hinokuma jingu'') and '''Kunikakasu Shrine''' (國懸神宮, ''Kunikakasu jingu'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine complex in {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama (city)|1=Wakayama (city)}} Prefecture, Japan. It is officially known simply as {{nihongo||日前宮|'''nichizengū'''}}. Its main festival is held annually on September 26. It shares the rank of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in the ''shinkai'' system. It was formerly an imperial shrine of the first rank (官幣大社, ''kanpei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It was also formerly the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}.
== Transportation == The shrine can be reached by {{ill|Nichizengū Station|en|Nichizengū Station|qid=Q11504968|lt=Nichizengū Station|1=Nichizengū Station}} on the Wakayama Electric Railway {{ill|Kishigawa Line|en|Kishigawa Line|qid=Q6416586|lt=Kishigawa Line|1=Kishigawa Line}}.<ref>{{cite news |title=人の手を借りる、たま駅長 貴志駅（和歌山県、和歌山電鉄貴志川線） |url=http://www.asahi.com/travel/hitoekigatari/TKY201102280171.html |accessdate=15 May 2020 |agency={{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}} |date=1 March 2011 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Overview == At the entrance, the Hinokuma Shrine is located on the left side, and the Kunikakasu Shrine is on the right side. Visiting the shrine in Wakayama City, along with [[Kamayama Shrine|Kamayama Jinja]] and [[Itakiso shrine|Itakiso Shrine]], is called "Sansha Mairi" (visiting three shrines).
== History == {{Unsourced section|date=October 2023}} The shrine is one of the oldest in Japan and has deep connections with mythology. The "Nihon Shoki" says that a mirror cast by Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto before the [[:en:Yata-no-Kagami|Yata-no-Kagami]], when Amaterasu Omikami hid in Amano-Iwato, is enshrined at the Hinokuma Shrine.
Initially, the shrine was located in Hamanomiya, Mezugori, Nagusa District.
In 1871 (Meiji 4), under the modern shrine ranking system, both shrines were designated as Kanpei Taisha. Currently, the shrine is an independent religious corporation not affiliated with organizations such as the Association of Shinto Shrines.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Jingū|en|List of Jingū|qid=Q120680118|lt=List of Jingū|1=List of Jingū}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://hinokuma-jingu.com Official website]
